commit.message:
feat: redesign editor forms with flat layout and StrataIconPicker

Redesign all four editor views (Agents, Skills, Projects, MCP Servers)
from the old two-column layout with bare TextBox and small-caps labels
to a clean, flat single-column form layout that fits within the
available viewport without overflow.

Changes:
- Replace card-based sections with flat form fields and Strata separator
  dividers between logical groups
- Add StrataIconPicker in a Flyout for emoji selection (Agents, Skills)
  replacing the cramped 72px TextBox
- Use Margin on content StackPanel instead of ScrollViewer Padding to
  prevent horizontal overflow of TextBox controls
- Tighten spacing to ensure all form sections (including Skills, MCP
  Servers, Tools) are visible without scrolling on standard window sizes
- Use localized strings for all field labels
- Keep StrataSetting/StrataSettingGroup only for Type/Enabled toggles in
  MCP Servers where the horizontal label+control layout is appropriate
- Fix StrataIconPicker.SelectIcon to use SetCurrentValue instead of
  SetValue to preserve TwoWay bindings (Strata submodule)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix window close hanging on some PCs

Move disk I/O out of OnClosing so the window disappears instantly.
Window bounds are captured in-memory during OnClosing, then persisted
in ShutdownRequested via Task.Run (avoids UI-thread deadlock when
_writeLock is held by an in-flight save). Both SaveAsync and
CopilotService.DisposeAsync now have timeouts to prevent infinite hangs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: organize Theorems/ into 7 subdirectories

Move 44 theorem files into thematic subdirectories:
- Solvability/ (6): core equation theory (ClosedForm, Invertibility, etc.)
- Clamping/ (5): value clamping (ClampLocal, ClosedFormBox, etc.)
- Iteration/ (5): convergence and contraction (JacobiContraction, BinaryAlpha, etc.)
- Approximation/ (8): near-binary, blur fusion, compositing errors
- Grid/ (10): grid-level and canonical builder theorems
- FixedPrecision/ (6): fixed-width arithmetic theorems
- Evaluation/ (4): metrics and gradient semantics

All imports updated. No functional changes.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor: extract Core.JacobiIteration from Theorems.JacobiContraction

Move 12 definitions (jacobiStep, jacobiIterate, jacobiSpectralRadius, etc.)
and 6 @[simp] accessor lemmas to Core.JacobiIteration. Theorems/JacobiContraction
now imports Core.JacobiIteration and re-exports all names for backward compat.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor: extract Core.ClosedForm, remove stale imports and dead files

Phase 0: Remove stale imports (Theorems.QuantizationBounds from
FixedPrecision/Coefficients, Theorems.ClosedFormMeanResidual from
FixedPrecision/LocalSolver). Delete non-compiling GlobalSystem.lean
and MultigridSpec.lean.

Phase 1-1: Extract closedFormSolution and related definitions from
Theorems/ClosedForm.lean into Core/ClosedForm.lean. Level/Jacobi.lean
now imports Core.ClosedForm instead of Theorems.ClosedForm, resolving
the layering violation. Backward compatibility maintained via export
in Theorems/ClosedForm.lean.

Also adds docs/refactoring-plan.md with full analysis and phased plan.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: dashboard active jobs use sidecar file for live tracking

The previous approach relied on gym.run OTEL spans which only export
when the run completes — useless for showing running jobs. Now uses
an active_runs.jsonl sidecar file:

- register_active_run() writes entry at suite start
- ActiveRunGuard removes entry on drop (suite finish or crash)
- read_active_runs() filters by /proc/PID to skip stale entries
- Dashboard reads sidecar file instead of gym.run spans
- ETA calculated from completed gym.case spans

commit.message:
Fix bite gap, font weight fallback, and fluid menu switching  for real

Root-cause analysis:
- BITE: 13px / 9 units = 1.44px per unit. The 1-unit bite gap at x=4 is
  only 1.44px wide  sub-pixel blending fills it in. Fix: logo rect is now
  18x22px (exactly 2px per unit), positioned at y=-2 so the stem clips
  above the bar while the body (grid rows 3-10) fills screen y=4..20.
  The bite gap is now a hard 2x2px white area, visible at any DPI.

- FONT WEIGHT: 'FontWeight.Bold' with the fallback chain
  'ChicagoFLF, Chicago, Geneva, Helvetica, Arial' causes Avalonia to search
  for a Bold variant of each family in order. ChicagoFLF Bold: not found.
  Chicago (system): not on Windows. Geneva: not on Windows. Helvetica: not
  on Windows. Arial Bold: FOUND  so Arial Bold was rendering instead of
  Chicago. Fix: specify ONLY the avares:// embedded FontFamily with no
  system fallbacks. SkiaSharp then synthesises fake-bold on ChicagoFLF.

- FLUID MENUS: The popup light-dismiss overlay intercepts the click that
  should switch to another menu. The overlay closes the current popup but
  the PointerPressed event never reaches the menu bar, so the new menu
  never opens  requiring a double click. Fix: OverlayDismissEventPassThrough
  = true makes that same click also route to underlying controls.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix oversized Apple logo, popup race condition crash, BeginMoveDrag guard

- Apple logo rect was 18x18px in a 20px bar, filling nearly the entire
  bar height. Reduced to 13x14px (logo centered at y=3, leaving 3px
  margins), matching the proportions of the original Mac menu bar.
- Fixed popup.Closed race condition: CloseMenu() now nulls _activePopup
  BEFORE calling p.Close(), so the synchronous Closed event sees null and
  returns via the new capture guard. Light-dismiss closes still work because
  the guard checks ReferenceEquals(_activePopup, capturedPopup).
  Previously, a light-dismiss close firing after OpenMenu() replaced the
  popup could overwrite _openMenuIndex and _activePopup with stale values,
  causing the next menu interaction to crash.
- Added try/catch around BeginMoveDrag() to prevent a platform-level failure
  from crashing the process when pointer state is unexpectedly stale.

Split NuGet mega-packages to stay under 500MB feed limit (#3584)

* Split NuGet mega-packages to stay under 500MB feed limit

The _NuGets, _NuGetsPreview, _Symbols, and _SymbolsPreview packages
exceeded the Azure DevOps 500MB size limit. Split each into:
  - .Managed (managed-only nupkgs, small)
  - .NativeAssets (native asset nupkgs, large)
  - Parent meta-package (depends on both sub-packages)

The download side (DownloadPackageAsync) already follows dependencies,
so consumers automatically get both sub-packages when requesting the
parent.

New nuspec templates:

fix: normalize && operands to boolean before i32.and

The && operator was compiled as i32.and (bitwise AND), which fails
when operands have value 2 (used for 'double' in last_expr_is_float).
Specifically, 2 & 1 = 0 (false) instead of the correct 1 (true),
to silently skip f64.convert_i32_s before f64.div.

Fix: for TOK_AMP_AMP, normalize both stack operands to 0/1 using
double-negation (i32.eqz; i32.eqz) before i32.and. This correctly
implements logical AND semantics for any non-boolean operand values.

   The creditor portal called /debt-service/api/v1/debts/claims for all

   The step indicator in all four wizard templates used 'step < currentStep'

fix: restore sequential encoder (batch encoder causes OOM)

Batch encoder across multiple requests caused GPU OOM when vLLM
scheduler sends many audio items at once. The encoder intermediates
(~700MB per 69s audio) compete with KV cache for GPU memory.

Sequential encoding is stable and proven correct. The encoder
(267ms per request) is not the primary throughput bottleneck when
encoder cache is enabled (default).

commit.message:
Phase 1: compound assign (*= /= %=), escape sequences, string concat, comma operator

Add *=, /=, %= compound assignment operators with full codegen support
for ident, deref, member, and subscript targets (including float and
unsigned handling).

Add missing C89 escape sequences: \a, \b, \f, \v, \?, \xHH hex, and
\NNN octal escapes in both character and string literals.

Implement adjacent string literal concatenation ("foo" "bar" -> "foobar")
with whitespace/newline skipping between segments.

Add comma operator at lowest precedence (bp=0). Function call arguments
and array initializers use parse_expr_bp(2) to avoid capturing commas.

All 54 tests pass (50 existing + 4 new), bootstrap validates
(s1.wat == s2.wat), and binary mode passes.

commit.message:
fix: add missing /counts endpoint to DebtController

The creditor portal called GET /debt-service/api/v1/debts/counts
but the endpoint did not exist. Spring routed the request to
@GetMapping(/{id}) treating 'counts' as a UUID path variable,
causing MethodArgumentTypeMismatchException. This opened the
circuit breaker in the portal, showing zero counts on the dashboard.

Added:
- ClaimCountsDto in debt-service with inRecovery/inHearing/rejected/zeroBalance
- DebtRepository: countByCreditorAndLifecycleState + countZeroBalanceByCreditor
- DebtService.getClaimCounts(UUID) interface method + implementation
- DebtController: @GetMapping('/counts') declared before /{id} to avoid ambiguity

Also fixed start-demo.ps1: corrected the 'Opret fordring' URL from
/fordring/ny to /fordring/opret.
